title: practical english usage - Part 235

We   do not use commas to separate the basic parts of a sentence (subject and 1     the basic sentence 296 punctua     tion:   c omma For commas in lists, ►    296.

undertake to make good any damage;. ..

regularly; that you arrange for all necessary cleaning to be carried out; that you You may use the sports facilities on condition that your subscription is paid are grammatically complex.

Semi-colons can also be used to separate items in a list, particularly when these 2    in lists Commas are not usually possible in cases like these (►   296.7).

It is a fine idea; let us hope that it is going to work.

Some people work best in the mornings; others do better in the evenings.

Semi-colons are not nearly as common as full stops or commas.

sentences are grammatically independent but the meaning is closely connected.

Semi-colons (;) are sometimes used instead of full stops, in cases where 1     instead of full stops 295 punctuation:        semi-colon In the words of Murphy's Law: 'Anything that can go wrong will go wrong.' hamlet :     Words, words, words.

:  What do you read, my lord?

(as in the text of a play, or when famous sayings are quoted).

And a colon is used when direct speech is introduced by a name or short phrase have contributed to the company's very gratifying results. First of all,...' Introducing his report for the year, the Chairman said: 'A number of factors But a long passage of direct speech may be introduced by a colon.

Stewart opened his eyes and said, 'Who's your beautiful friend?' Normally, direct speech is introduced by a comma in writing.

Written Texts   Section     26 grammar •  296  punctuation: comma but not work force, (          Ordersbegpn to dry upr most companies started. . .) or Orders began to dry up; most companies started to reduce their Orders began to dry up. Most companies started to reduce their work force.

(comma and conjunction) Orders began to dry up, and most companies reduced their work force.

stop or a semi-colon, but not a comma. Compare: Between grammatically separate sentences (with no conjunction), we  use a full 7 between       grammatically          separate       sentences had disappeared for good.

They did not understand that the economic conditions that had existed earlier relative pronoun).

A  useful rule: we  do not put a comma  before that (conjunction or how serious...) not    o No one knew how serious the situation would become, (     N  one knew, not (     Many-eommentators-deel&redrtkat-tke-eeenemy-...) Many commentators declared that the economy was in serious trouble.

We   do not put commas after verbs of saying, thinking, etc in indirect speech.

6 indirect      speech When the export markets began to contract, the situation changed.

\Ne usually use a comma if we  start with the conjunction.

markets began to contract.

The situation changed noticeably for the worse after 1932, when the export The situation changed when the export markets began to contract.

in longer sentences. Compare: We   often put commas in sentences with conjunctions, especially 5  sentences       with    conjunctions Andreas Bergmeister, who established the dock workers' union,...

However, we  do use commas to separate non-identifying expressions (►   234).

labourers, in the north eastern docks) not the mainly foreign labourers in the north-eastern docks (     the matnfyforeign not the mainly foreign labourers (     fhe-mainly-fbreignrl^bourers) go with it.

We   do not usually separate a noun from the adjectives or other expressions that 4 noun    phrases 1930s, was steadily improving.

The standard of living of the dock workers, during the late 1920s and early we  may put commas before and after them.

When  adverbial expressions come between or inside parts of the basic sentence, 3  inside     the   basic    sentence late 1920s and early 1930s.

The standard of living of the dock workers was slowly improving during the basic sentence.

We   do not so often put commas when adverbial expressions come  after the grammar •  296  punctuation: comma or 3.5 = three point five   three and a half(xoi three-com nw-five) We   do not use commas in decimals (►  322.1).
